Load and inspect the crime data for 2021-2025

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import altair as alt
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point
import os

Crime = pd.read_csv('data/raw-data/InitialCrimes.csv')

Crime.head()

pd.set_option("display.max_columns", None)
pd.set_option("display.max_colwidth", None)
pd.set_option("display.width", None)

Crime.head(5)
```

Join the crime data with zip code data and drop crimes with missing zip codes.

```{python}
Crime = Crime.dropna(subset=["Latitude", "Longitude"])

Crime_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    Crime,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(Crime['Longitude'], Crime['Latitude']),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

zip_codes = gpd.read_file("data/raw-data/ZC/ZC.shp")

zip_codes = zip_codes.to_crs(Crime_gdf.crs)

Crime_with_zip = gpd.sjoin(
    Crime_gdf,
    zip_codes,
    how="left",
    predicate="within"
)

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime_with_zip["zip"]

missing = Crime["Zip Code"].isna().sum()
print(f"{missing} crimes do not have a ZIP code.")

Crime = Crime.dropna(subset=["Zip Code"])
```

Merge the crime data with the population data.
```{python}
Crime["Date"] = pd.to_datetime(Crime["Date"])

Population = pd.read_csv("data/raw-data/PopulationZC.csv")
Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].astype(str)
Population["GEOID"] = Population["GEOID"].astype(str)
Crime = Crime.merge(
    Population[["GEOID", "POP_2019-2023"]],
    left_on="Zip Code",
    right_on="GEOID",
    how="left"
)
Crime = Crime.drop(columns=["GEOID"])
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"POP_2019-2023": "Population"})
```

Chart monthly crime in Chicago over time.

```{python}
monthly_crime = (
    Crime
    .set_index("Date")
    .resample("M")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
)

chart1 = alt.Chart(monthly_crime).mark_line().encode(
    x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("CrimeCount:Q", title="Number of Crimes")
).properties(
    width=700,
    height=400,
    title="Monthly Crime in Chicago (2021–2025)"
)

chart1
```

Combine the two sexual assault categories, and make a function to plot different crime types by month over time.

```{python}
Crime["Primary Type"] = Crime["Primary Type"].replace({
    "CRIM SEXUAL ASSAULT": "CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT"
})

def crime_trend(crime_type):
    
    subset = Crime[Crime["Primary Type"] == crime_type]
    
    monthly_counts = (
        subset
        .set_index("Date")
        .resample("M")
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
    )
    
    chart = alt.Chart(monthly_counts).mark_line().encode(
        x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
        y=alt.Y("CrimeCount:Q", title="Number of Crimes")
    ).properties(
        width=700,
        height=400,
        title=f"Monthly {crime_type} Crimes in Chicago"
    )
    
    return chart
```

Generate monthly crime graphs for each crime type.

```{python}
crime_trend("ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_trend("BATTERY")
```

```{python}
crime_trend("CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_trend("HOMICIDE")
```

Calculate the crime rate in each zip code and divide the data into quintiles by crime rate. Then plot monthly crime rates by quintile.
```{python}
Crime["Population"] = (
    Crime["Population"]
    .str.replace(",", "", regex=False)
    .astype(float)
)

Crime["Population"] = pd.to_numeric(Crime["Population"])

zip_crime_counts = (
    Crime.groupby("Zip Code")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="TotalCrimes")
)

zip_population = Crime[["Zip Code", "Population"]].drop_duplicates()

zip_stats = zip_crime_counts.merge(zip_population, on="Zip Code")

zip_stats["CrimeRate"] = zip_stats["TotalCrimes"] / zip_stats["Population"] * 1000

zip_stats["Quintile"] = pd.qcut(
    zip_stats["CrimeRate"],
    5,
    labels=["Q1 Lowest", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 Highest"]
)

Crime = Crime.merge(
    zip_stats[["Zip Code", "Quintile"]],
    on="Zip Code",
    how="left"
)

monthly_quintile = (
    Crime
    .set_index("Date")
    .groupby("Quintile")
    .resample("M")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
)

pop_by_quintile = (
    zip_stats.groupby("Quintile")["Population"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

monthly_quintile = monthly_quintile.merge(
    pop_by_quintile,
    on="Quintile"
)

monthly_quintile["CrimeRate"] = (
    monthly_quintile["CrimeCount"] / monthly_quintile["Population"] * 1000
)

chart2 = alt.Chart(monthly_quintile).mark_line().encode(
    x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("CrimeRate:Q", title="Crime Rate (per 1,000 Residents)"),
    color=alt.Color(
        "Quintile:N",
        scale=alt.Scale(range=[
            "#08306B",  
            "#2171B5",
            "#6BAED6",
            "#BDD7E7",
            "#C6DBEF"   
        ]),
        title="Zip Code Crime Rate Quintile")
).properties(
    width=700,
    height=400,
    title="Monthly Crime Rates by Zip Code Crime Rate Quintile"
)

chart2
```

Plot the total monthly crime rate over time in Chicago.
```{python}
total_population = (
    Crime[["Zip Code", "Population"]]
    .drop_duplicates()
    ["Population"]
    .sum()
)

monthly_total = (
    Crime
    .set_index("Date")
    .resample("M")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
)

monthly_total["CrimeRate"] = (
    monthly_total["CrimeCount"] / total_population * 1000
)

chart3 = alt.Chart(monthly_total).mark_line(color="#08306B").encode(
    x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("CrimeRate:Q", title="Crime Rate (per 1,000 residents)")
).properties(
    width=700,
    height=400,
    title="Monthly Crime Rate in Chicago (2021–2025)"
)

chart3
```

Define a function to plot the crime rate for any crime type over time.
```{python}
def crime_rate_type(crime_type):

    subset = Crime[Crime["Primary Type"] == crime_type]

    monthly = (
        subset
        .set_index("Date")
        .resample("M")
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
    )

    total_population = (
        Crime[["Zip Code", "Population"]]
        .drop_duplicates()["Population"]
        .sum()
    )

    monthly["CrimeRate"] = monthly["CrimeCount"] / total_population * 1000

    chart = alt.Chart(monthly).mark_line(color="#08306B").encode(
        x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
        y=alt.Y("CrimeRate:Q", title="Crimes per 1,000 Residents")
    ).properties(
        width=700,
        height=400,
        title=f"Monthly {crime_type} Crime Rate"
    )

    return chart
```

Plot the crime rates for each crime type

```{python}
crime_rate_type("ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_rate_type("BATTERY")
```

```{python}
crime_rate_type("CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_rate_type("HOMICIDE")
```

Define a function to split the zip codes into quintiles by crime rate for a specific crime type and then graph the crime rate for that type in each quintile.

```{python}
def crime_quintile_trend(crime_type):

    subset = Crime[Crime["Primary Type"] == crime_type].copy()

    # Remove the previously-created quintile column if it exists
    if "Quintile" in subset.columns:
        subset = subset.drop(columns=["Quintile"])

    # Count crimes of this type by ZIP
    zip_counts = (
        subset.groupby("Zip Code")
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="TotalCrimes")
    )

    # ZIP populations
    zip_pop = Crime[["Zip Code", "Population"]].drop_duplicates()

    # Merge so ZIPs with zero crimes still appear
    zip_stats = zip_pop.merge(zip_counts, on="Zip Code", how="left")

    zip_stats["TotalCrimes"] = zip_stats["TotalCrimes"].fillna(0)

    # Crime rate per 1,000 residents
    zip_stats["CrimeRate"] = zip_stats["TotalCrimes"] / zip_stats["Population"] * 1000

    # Create quintiles for this crime type
    zip_stats["Quintile"] = pd.qcut(
        zip_stats["CrimeRate"],
        5,
        labels=["Q1 Lowest", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 Highest"]
    )

    # Attach quintile to crimes
    subset = subset.merge(
        zip_stats[["Zip Code", "Quintile"]],
        on="Zip Code",
        how="left"
    )

    # Monthly crime counts by quintile
    monthly = (
        subset
        .set_index("Date")
        .groupby("Quintile")
        .resample("M")
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="CrimeCount")
    )

    # Population per quintile
    pop_by_quintile = (
        zip_stats.groupby("Quintile")["Population"]
        .sum()
        .reset_index()
    )

    monthly = monthly.merge(pop_by_quintile, on="Quintile")

    monthly["CrimeRate"] = monthly["CrimeCount"] / monthly["Population"] * 1000

    chart = alt.Chart(monthly).mark_line().encode(
        x=alt.X("Date:T", title="Month"),
        y=alt.Y("CrimeRate:Q", title="Crimes per 1,000 Residents"),
        color=alt.Color(
            "Quintile:N",
            scale=alt.Scale(range=[
                "#08306B",
                "#2171B5",
                "#6BAED6",
                "#BDD7E7",
                "#C6DBEF"
            ]),
            title="Zip Code Crime Rate Quintile"
        )
    ).properties(
        width=700,
        height=400,
        title=f"Monthly {crime_type} Crime Rates by Zip Code Quintile"
    )

    return chart
```

Plot quintile graphs for each crime type 

```{python}
crime_quintile_trend("ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_quintile_trend("BATTERY")
```

```{python}
crime_quintile_trend("CRIMINAL SEXUAL ASSAULT")
```

```{python}
crime_quintile_trend("HOMICIDE")
```

Merge the crime data with the poor mental health, social/emotional support, and child opportunity index indicators.
```{python}
PMHSESCOI = pd.read_csv("data/raw-data/PMHSESCOI.csv")

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].astype(str)
PMHSESCOI["GEOID"] = PMHSESCOI["GEOID"].astype(str)

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.strip()
PMHSESCOI["GEOID"] = PMHSESCOI["GEOID"].str.strip()

PMHSESCOI["GEOID"] = PMHSESCOI["GEOID"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)

Crime = Crime.merge(
    PMHSESCOI[["GEOID","PMH_2023","SES_2023","CHACFZN_2023"]],
    left_on="Zip Code",
    right_on="GEOID",
    how="left"
)

Crime = Crime.drop(columns=["GEOID"])
```

Merge the crime data with the hardship index data.
```{python}
HI = pd.read_csv("data/raw-data/HI.csv")

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].astype(str)
HI["GEOID"] = HI["GEOID"].astype(str)

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.strip()
HI["GEOID"] = HI["GEOID"].str.strip()

HI["GEOID"] = HI["GEOID"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)

Crime = Crime.merge(
    HI[["GEOID", "HDX_2019-2023"]],
    left_on="Zip Code",
    right_on="GEOID",
    how="left"
)

Crime = Crime.drop(columns=["GEOID"])
```

Merge the crime date with the behavioral-health hospitalization data.
```{python}
BHH = pd.read_csv("data/raw-data/BHH.csv")

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].astype(str)
BHH["GEOID"] = BHH["GEOID"].astype(str)

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.strip()
BHH["GEOID"] = BHH["GEOID"].str.strip()

BHH["GEOID"] = BHH["GEOID"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)

Crime = Crime.merge(
    BHH[["GEOID", "HDBHR_2023"]],
    left_on="Zip Code",
    right_on="GEOID",
    how="left"
)

Crime = Crime.drop(columns=["GEOID"])
```

Merge the crime data with the social vulnerability index data.
```{python}
SVI = pd.read_csv("data/raw-data/SVI_2022.csv")

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].astype(str)
SVI["GEOID"] = SVI["GEOID"].astype(str)

Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.strip()
SVI["GEOID"] = SVI["GEOID"].str.strip()

SVI["GEOID"] = SVI["GEOID"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
Crime["Zip Code"] = Crime["Zip Code"].str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)

Crime = Crime.merge(
    SVI[["GEOID", "SVI_2022"]],
    left_on="Zip Code",
    right_on="GEOID",
    how="left"
)

Crime = Crime.drop(columns=["GEOID"])
```

Adjust column names for new variables.
```{python}
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"PMH_2023": "Poor Mental Health"})
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"SES_2023": "Lacking Social/Emotional Support"})
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"CHACFZN_2023": "Childhood Opportunity Index"})
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"HDX_2019-2023": "Hardship Index"})
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"HDBHR_2023": "Behavioral Health-related Hospitalizations"})
Crime = Crime.rename(columns={"SVI_2022": "Social Vulnerability Index"})
Crime
```

Subset the data to 2023 and calculate the crime rate by zip code within that year.
```{python}
Crime_2023 = Crime[Crime["Date"].dt.year == 2023].copy()

zip_crime_counts = (
    Crime_2023.groupby("Zip Code")
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="TotalCrimes")
)

Crime_2023 = Crime_2023.merge(
    zip_crime_counts,
    on="Zip Code",
    how="left"
)

Crime_2023["CrimeRate"] = Crime_2023["TotalCrimes"] / Crime_2023["Population"] * 1000

Crime_2023 = Crime_2023.drop(columns=["TotalCrimes"])
```

Save the Crime dataset in derived-data.
```{python}
Crime.to_csv("data/derived-data/Crime.csv", index=False)
```

Aggregate the data and group by zip code. Then calculate Pearson correlation coefficients between the crime rate and the other variables.
```{python}
zip_data = (
    Crime_2023.groupby("Zip Code")
    .agg({
        "CrimeRate": "first",
        "Poor Mental Health": "first",
        "Lacking Social/Emotional Support": "first",
        "Childhood Opportunity Index": "first",
        "Behavioral Health-related Hospitalizations": "first",
        "Hardship Index": "first",
        "Social Vulnerability Index": "first"
    })
    .reset_index()
)

cols = [
    "CrimeRate",
    "Poor Mental Health",
    "Lacking Social/Emotional Support",
    "Childhood Opportunity Index",
    "Behavioral Health-related Hospitalizations",
    "Hardship Index",
    "Social Vulnerability Index"
]

zip_data.to_csv(os.path.join("data/", "derived-data/zip_data.csv"), index=False)

zip_data[cols] = zip_data[cols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")

corr = zip_data.corr(numeric_only=True)["CrimeRate"].sort_values(ascending=False)
print(corr)
```

Plot crime rate versus hardship index to identify outliers.
```{python}
alt.Chart(zip_data).mark_circle(size=80).encode(
    x="Hardship Index:Q",
    y=alt.Y("CrimeRate:Q", title="Crime Rate"),
).properties(
    title="Crime Rate by Hardship Index for Chicago Zip Codes"
    )
```

Remove the three outlier zip codes, and generate new correlation coefficients
```{python}
zip_data_clean = zip_data[zip_data["CrimeRate"] <= 150]

corr = zip_data_clean.corr(numeric_only=True)["CrimeRate"].sort_values(ascending=False)
print(corr)


```

View the outlier zip codes to identify their location.
```{python}
outliers = zip_data[zip_data["CrimeRate"] > 150]
outliers
```

Calculate Spearman coefficients and plot them with the Pearson coefficients.
```{python}
Spearman = zip_data_clean.corr(method="spearman", numeric_only=True)["CrimeRate"]
Spearman

corr = corr.reset_index()
Spearman = Spearman.reset_index()

corr.columns = ["Indicator", "Pearson"]
Spearman.columns = ["Indicator", "Spearman"]

corr_clean = corr[corr["Indicator"] != "CrimeRate"]
spearman_clean = Spearman[Spearman["Indicator"] != "CrimeRate"]

corr_clean["Pearson"] = corr_clean["Pearson"].abs()
spearman_clean["Spearman"] = spearman_clean["Spearman"].abs()

combined = corr_clean.merge(spearman_clean, on="Indicator")

combined = combined.sort_values("Pearson", ascending=False)

plot_data = combined.melt(
    id_vars="Indicator",
    var_name="Correlation Type",
    value_name="Coefficient"
)

indicator_order = combined["Indicator"].tolist()

chart = alt.Chart(plot_data).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("Indicator:N", sort=indicator_order, title="Health Indicator", axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=-30)),
    y=alt.Y("Coefficient:Q", title="Correlation with Crime Rate"),
    color="Correlation Type:N",
    xOffset="Correlation Type"
).properties(
    title="Pearson vs Spearman Absolute Value Correlation Coefficient with Crime Rate",
    width=650,
    height=400
)
```

Define function to make heatmap of Chicago for a given variable.
```{python}
def chicago_zip_heatmap(var, label):
    """
    var: string, column name in zip_data to plot
    label: string, used for legend and plot title
    """
    map_data = gpd.read_file("data/raw-data/ZC/ZC.shp")
  
    map_data["zip"] = map_data["zip"].astype(str)
    zip_data["Zip Code"] = zip_data["Zip Code"].astype(str)
    
  
    map_data["zip"] = map_data["zip"].str.strip()
    zip_data["Zip Code"] = zip_data["Zip Code"].str.strip()
    
  
    map_data_merged = map_data.merge(
        zip_data,
        left_on="zip",
        right_on="Zip Code",
        how="left"
    )
    
    
    map_json = map_data_merged.__geo_interface__

    

    chart = alt.Chart(alt.Data(values=map_json["features"])).mark_geoshape(
        stroke='black',
        strokeWidth=1.5
    ).encode(
        color = alt.Color(
    f"properties.{var}:Q",
    scale=alt.Scale(scheme="blues", type="quantile"),
    title=label,
    legend=alt.Legend(
        format=".1f",             
        labelExpr="datum.label"   
    )
),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("properties.zip:N", title="ZIP Code"),
            alt.Tooltip(f"properties.{var}:Q", title=label)
        ]
    ).properties(
        width=700,
        height=700,
        title=f"{label} across Chicago by Zip Code"
    ).project("mercator")
    
    return chart
```

Make a heatmap of the crime rate across Chicago.
```{python}
chicago_zip_heatmap("CrimeRate", "Crime Rate")
```
